metadata.use_case:
I need help designing a schema for analyzing public comments submitted to a federal regulatory docket regarding medical payment products (medical credit cards and loans). These comments were submitted in response to a joint Request for Information (RFI) from the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB), Department of Health and Human Services (HHS), and Department of the Treasury.

The comments come from diverse stakeholders:
- Individual patients sharing personal experiences with medical cre

representative_narrative_snippets: Human Rights Watch strongly supports the agencies’ efforts to better regulate the rights-impacting practices of private entities.
regulatory_mandate_quotes: Human Rights Watch strongly supports the agencies’ efforts to better regulate the rights-impacting practices of private entities.

representative_narrative_snippets: Neighborhood Trust Financial Partners’ Comment in Response to Request for Information Regarding Medical Payment Products.
Care Credit offered a promo for 0% financing for 24 months, but then an interest rate of 22.9% and above, with severe penalty APR.
I wish to record the issues I have with the Care Credit credit card issued by Synchrony Bank.
My full comment is too long to fit into the online form's 5,000 character limit.
The U.S. has some of the highest healthcare costs in the world, which can lead to significant financial burdens on patients.
The issues surrounding medical debt and healthcare costs in America are complex and interconnected.
Comment from National Consumer Law Center, Americans for Financial Reform Education Fund, and Georgia Watch
Comment from U.S. Chamber of Commerce
I work in HR, and even trying to explain to people how insurance works is hard.
it is so hard to review the forms and understand what happened.
Comment submitted by Allan Share
Alberto B. Casellas EVP & CEO, Health & Wellness Synchrony
See Attached
comments from the American Society of Plastic Surgeons in response to the Agency's Request for Information
comment letter filed on behalf of California Attorney General Rob Bonta.
A Center at the University of the District of Columbia David A. Clarke School of Law
users had much higher rates of 1) problems paying for medical care; 2) being contacted by a collections agency; 3) putting off medical care due to costs
regulatory_mandate_quotes: I wish to applaud the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau for looking into medical payment products.

representative_narrative_snippets: Federal Register / Vol. 88, No. 132 / Wednesday, July 12, 2023 / Notices
See Attached
See Attached
See Attached
See Attached
Comment submitted by Anders Gilberg (MGMA)
Comment from AHIP
RIP Medical Debt regarding the impact of medical debt on consumer financial stability.
Comment from American Hospital Association (AHA) regarding provider perspectives.
Comment from ACA International representing the accounts receivable management industry.
The medical billing practices that consumers (patients) must be a part of (be victims of) are for the most part unknown and mysterious and out of the consumers (patients) control.
Taking advantage of consumers (patients) when they are so vulnerable is IMMORAL.
regulatory_mandate_quotes: If there ever was a role for the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to protect consumers, this IS IT!

representative_narrative_snippets: Please see the attached comments of the Dental Trade Alliance.
I went for a few months without health insurance because I was changing jobs and it wasn't pretty.
If I didn't have regular credit cards, I would be in big trouble.
regulatory_mandate_quotes: I don't know what is going on but this whole system needs to be looked at.

representative_narrative_snippets: See attached file(s)
Please find attached comments from the Academy of Doctors of Audiology.
I actually find the Care Credit option of payment helpful.
I've found it to be helpful in stretching my budget and necessary for dental expenses or emergency bills.
My only relationship comes from emergency care under the Veterans Administration programs & they have been great.
I haven't done the treatment I need yet because I can't pay for this over the course of a year and then I would have onerous credit terms.
the issue I find in my town is not being able to find a doctor or dentist who will accept Medicaid, especially for dental care.
We don't need these products, don't want these products, and will not involve ourselves by obtaining or utilizing these products.
Thomas D. Policelli CEO, HPS/PayMedix
Please see attached comments from PayZen. Thank you.
Re: Request for Information Regarding Specialty Medical Payment Products
we submit the attached comments on behalf of the more than 100,000 Georgians burdened with medical debt.
We are pleased that your Agencies are seeking to understand the effects that medical credit cards, loans, and other financial products have on patients.
regulatory_mandate_quotes: Seeking to understand the effects that medical credit cards, loans, and other financial products have on patients and the healthcare system.

representative_narrative_snippets: Assistive technology helps to meet the medical need of a person who has a disability or a medical condition.
A modified vehicle is a form of assistive technology that helps to address the medical needs for those who rely on private transportation.
The CARE CREDIT medical credit card has kept me in debt.
I pray that my service pet doesn’t get sick again because the interest rate is so high that I’m in debt immediately.
These products are nothing short of a rip-off.
Just letting them charge whatever they can get away with is crazy.
regulatory_mandate_quotes: At the very, very least, our regulators should require that these products stay within the guidelines and, more importantly, that prices for the services match Medicare levels.

representative_narrative_snippets: I feel the dental practitioner may have received a 'kickback' for pushing me in the direction of a particular loan agency.
Them financing interest was greatly bloated.
regulatory_mandate_quotes: Better still is to provide a government program to address health needs.

representative_narrative_snippets: Please see attached comments from the American Animal Hospital Association.
I was told I did not qualify because I was disabled.
I was told they would refund the premiums... that was a lie.
I can’t survive on ssd so I’m claiming bankruptcy.
I’ve had 6 tummy ops, hashimotos, slow teeth loss, ortho issues, toe issues and pvcs with heart aneurysm.
regulatory_mandate_quotes: Why can’t I who lives at doctors dentists pharmacists operating rooms have Medicaid back secondary to Medicare?

representative_narrative_snippets: The stress and lack of insurance caused more damage than good.
I drove myself into the hospital via 6 weeks of round trips... for that job.
I have used my cc to pay for eye exams and dental care for years because most insurance doesn’t cover them correctly.
stop them from ruining o credit report of the patient
regulatory_mandate_quotes: make these billing companies bill the correct agency and stop them from ruining o credit report of the patient

representative_narrative_snippets: Duplicate to CFPB-2023-0038-0048
very informative previously payment for revision labs test deflation augmentation
Manuel A. Cordero, DDS, CPH <ceo@hdassoc.org>
Cathy Christensen <cathy@americanmedspa.org>
August 31, 2023 Consumer Financial Protection Bureau 1700 G Street NW
Please see attached comments from the American Dental Association.
Please see the attached comment from the American Veterinary Medical Association.
I have a bill In collections with Penn Credit for Broward Health.
Need help paying bill or remove from collections with payment plan to attain an apartment.
regulatory_mandate_quotes: Need help paying bill or remove from collections with payment plan to attain an apartment.

representative_narrative_snippets: Patients being treatment for behavioral health conditions may be more likely than others to have medical debt and may have trouble finding treatment fully covered by insurance
regulatory_mandate_quotes: I write to encourage CFPB, Treasury and HHS to further study the implications of use of such medical payment products

representative_narrative_snippets: RE: Docket No. CFPB–2023–0038
This new practice of just signing patients up on admittance into finance contracts will put more people in financial jeopardy
most bankruptcies are related to medical debt
This predatory activity of pushing patients into finance contracts will only make matters worse for patients and their families
Hospitals used to set up payment plans when needed, often at no interest
Most bankruptcies in the US are related to medical debt. This predatory activity of pushing patients into finance contracts will only make matters worse
If there were universal healthcare in the US we would not be having a discussion on financing health care debt
Governing for Impact submitted via www.regulations.gov to Administrator Chiquita Brooks-LaSure.
Being placed in collections and no options becoming homeless.
maybe if their funding is cut by sponsors maybe they can understand the position many patients are placed in.
Comment submitted was flagged as being materially false, fictitious or fraudulent representation.
It would be good if there was way to pay for college that was less userous and more equitable.
Or a way to pay back loans that didn't crowd or intermingle with your credit rating.
See Attached
From: Trina Calloway To: MedicalDebtRFI_2023
The use of Care Credit allowed me to have much needed dental work and pay for it over time.
I have successfully used the product by paying as agreed and in full at the conclusion of the term.
A significant issue that is common amongst Flexible Spending Accounts is that many have a use-it-or-lose-it provision.
I felt pressured into signing up for CareCredit by an emergency vet clinic.
There was an “interest free” period of only 6 months... I have been charged over $1,300 in interest.
accidentally posted a test comment.
I hope that any proposed and final regulations prioritize the needs and actions of actual people.
regulatory_mandate_quotes: I hope that any proposed and final regulations prioritize the needs and actions of actual people.

representative_narrative_snippets: The Los Angeles County Department of Public Health is deeply committed to protecting the health and well-being of our residents.
Medical expenses are not discretionary spending. Debt from life saving care should be treated differently than other debit.
Stress caused by collection calls/unpaid bills is the least conducive thing to recovery from illness or injury.
Response to Request for Information Regarding Medical Payment Products
Medical debt is a glaring manifestation of the deeply flawed and corrupt health care system in our country.
It is an egregious injustice that leaves countless individuals drowning in an insurmountable burden.
regulatory_mandate_quotes: This dire situation demands immediate attention and concrete actions to rectify the systemic issues plaguing our health care system.

representative_narrative_snippets: Alberto B. Casellas, EVP & CEO, Health & Wellness, Synchrony
I was treated at PeaceHealth St. Joseph Hospital... my bills were over $1 million.
I will need chiropractic, aftercare...
They bated me and painted the card like it was perfect for me.
My balance of $700 not significantly decreased despite making consistent and timely payments.
regulatory_mandate_quotes: I am writing to express my extreme dissatisfaction with the services provided by Care Credit.

representative_narrative_snippets: Even with insurance, I don't always go to my doctor when it would probably be a good idea because my deductible is a few thousand dollars.
Newly retired and likely to need more medical care in the coming years.
Deferred payment plans provide a significant level of flexibility and financial relief to individuals facing unexpected expenses.
As a responsible credit card user, I believe that these options play a crucial role.
regulatory_mandate_quotes: express my sincere concern and request regarding the availability of deferred payment options on credit cards.

representative_narrative_snippets: We fight for a world in which, without exception, public policies and investments remove barriers for most impacted children and families.
advocate for the development and implementation of data-driven public policy.
regulatory_mandate_quotes: public policies and investments remove barriers for most impacted children and families and improve well-being.

representative_narrative_snippets: As an immuno-compromised senior, the issue of financing health care debt is of great concern to me.
This predatory activity of pushing patients into finance contracts will only make matters worse for patients and their families and increase the number of bankruptcies.
My dentist has offered me a medical credit card to help finance a $5,000 medical dental repair that is not covered by my insurance.
My dentist acted offended that I would not use the credit card financing.
My organization partners with a local bank to provide financing to our patients for up to 72 months at an interest rate of between 4%-6%.
Offering the financing to our patients is not our first option by any means, it is just there as another option.
regulatory_mandate_quotes: I'd be careful to lump them all into the same category.

representative_narrative_snippets: See Attached
I have been a huge difference for the Finance committee and I testified a couple of times for it
I told the financial lady I would only pay the Medicare price for the visit. One month later I was billed $2,664 for an EKG and some labs.
They billed me for additional codes outside of the ER visit.
No one should feel like they would rather risk their health, their life at times, just because they're unfamiliar with the healthcare system.
It is unconscionable that healthcare admins/non-profit hospitals... withhold VITAL information from their charges.
regulatory_mandate_quotes: It is necessary to broaden the public's knowledge with regard to their own health

representative_narrative_snippets: I've suddenly got a letter from a collections agency or it showed up on my credit report, that I suddenly owe a co-pay.
I've had sudden collection demands for $400, a co-pay from Advocate Christ Hospital for spinal surgery in 2008.
I have insurance, and my bill was still out of control. Neither hospital, nor any clinic I've been to since has informed me of financial aid options.
If I miss a deadline once because of my Neurodiversity, I'm back on the chopping block with collections.
My inability to pay for healthcare has prevented me from seeking out even basic appointments like physicals and check ups.
Medical debt is a crisis all over the United States. Hospitals should not be allowed to claim not for profit status while refusing to provide charity care.
regulatory_mandate_quotes: The federal government needs to act urgently to protect the wellbeing of everyday Americans from predatory hospital systems.

representative_narrative_snippets: The following are five concrete proposals aimed at preventing patients from encountering unaffordable healthcare costs
regulatory_mandate_quotes: Consider alleviating providers from the collection of cost-sharing by encouraging health plans... to directly collect the required cost-sharing

representative_narrative_snippets: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
I was a graduate student in college without any health care or dental care coverage when I had one of my molars collapse on me.
The dentist recommended that I open a Care Credit card for the $900 it would cost to have the work done.
Please see the attached comments.
regulatory_mandate_quotes: The Honorable Chiquita Brooks-LaSure Administrator Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services

representative_narrative_snippets: Please see the attached comment from the National Dental Association.
After almost a year and many different expensive test they gave me my diagnosis of stage 4 cancer and a very glim prognosis.
I believe they took advantage of me and my insurance.
Our policy has a $5,000 per year out-of-pocket maximum... this feels more like an out-of-pocket expectation or obligation in order to get health care.
Our gross income last year was $63,000, so $5,000 makes a difference to our household budget.
Subject: Ban Predatory Medical Payment Products
The CFPB should ban deferred interest on medical credit cards, which are often marketed at the point of care when patients are most vulnerable.
regulatory_mandate_quotes: The CFPB should use its authority to ban predatory deferred interest and require hospitals to screen for financial assistance before offering credit products.

representative_narrative_snippets: Actual bills from hospitals actually easily cover the cost of a brand new car-something the vast majority of the people our congress represents cannot afford !!
This whole 'medical payment' scheme just causes me more money with absolutely nothing to show for it.
Care Credit materials seemed omnipresent in his office, with posters and pamphlets prominently displayed.
I made a regrettable decision when I chose to utilize Care Credit... I found myself burdened with a high-interest rate of 26.99%.
regulatory_mandate_quotes: I urge you to take decisive action to protect consumers from the predatory practices of medical credit card companies like Care Credit.
